title: Add --header option to systemd generate
body:
Proposed an implementation for #14072 
~~I renamed the option to `--extra-header` since the service file would still have the additional header even if `--no-header` is specified.~~


#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
Add `--header` option to `podman generate systemd`
`--no-header` no longer generates the service name header
```

Needs a test, but core code LGTM

Updated with tests.
Examples:

```
$ podman generate systemd $CTR
# container-0df6882244b7f9e18909cc28dda167bdead56ec351274b5868a404da72b74b82.service
# autogenerated by Podman 4.2.0-dev
# Thu Aug 25 01:59:51 CST 2022

[Unit]
...
```

```
$ podman generate systemd $CTR --no-header
[Unit]
...
```

```
$ podman generate systemd $CTR --header '# Foo'
# Foo

[Unit]
...
```

```
$ podman generate systemd $CTR --header '# Foo' --no-header
Error: cannot use --header with --no-header
```
Can you squash all of your commits. and repush.
Could someone review?

Your test is failing.

https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/6020431877505024/logs/main.log

Shows tests failing on generated systemd unit files.

Fixed and has passed localunit on my machine.

It's my first time using Ginkgo, so I was confused by PASS-PASS-PASS and then suddenly FAIL. But the failure, in fact, happened earlier.

@vrothberg How to run a single integration test? (The full test takes too long)
